Identically Distributed vs. i.i.d.
Suppose that we have two random variables X and Y.
Instructor
X and Y are identically distributed if the distribution of X is the same as the distribution of Y.
X and Y are independent and identically distributed (i.i.d.) if:
X and Y have the same distribution, and
.
Knowing the outcome of X does not influence your belief
of the outcome of Y, and vice versa ("X and Y are independent.")

How does this dice example relate to investments?
Instructor
Often, you're advised to diversify investments to reduce risk. Don't put all $ in one investment.
In other words, don't gamble everything on the first dice roll. Spread your $ over the rolls.
Diversification reduces the variance of your portfolio of investments.
This is related to why we pay for insurance. We pay premiums (i.e ., reduce our expected value)
in exchange for help in a catastrophe (i.e ., greatly reduced variance). "Spread the risk"
